summary: Sweep B.1 TC4 hotfix VERIFIED — empty-error bug FIXED. Created /app/backend/tests/test_sweep_b1_tc4_hotfix.py (9 tests, ALL PASS). NZ + CA + SG AI-draft jobs now reliably reach terminal state in ~138s with a fully meaningful error: 'RuntimeError: All AI providers failed. Last error: TimeoutError: anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 exceeded 45s budget'. This proves: (1) per-model timeout caps work (Sonnet 90s → Haiku 45s instant fallback), (2) last_err is preserved + surfaced via type(exc).__name__ formatting, (3) outer 180s budget is sufficient — no more empty error strings, no more 3-min hangs, no more workflow_id=None mystery failures. TC5 seeded fastpath also confirmed: /api/ai-workflow/generate returned source='seeded_verified' in 0.12s. Backend logs show clean exception traces, NO 5xx responses from country_workflows or ai_workflow endpoints.

minor.issue: Both Sonnet 4.5 and Haiku 4.5 currently time out for AU/CA/NZ/SG generation in this environment — likely Emergent LLM proxy slowness or upstream Anthropic rate-limit on the universal key. Hotfix successfully SURFACES this rather than swallowing it. Investigate proxy latency or consider lowering MIN_DOCS_PER_STEP/prompt size to fit Haiku 45s budget.

action_items: PRIMARY HOTFIX OBJECTIVE ACHIEVED — empty-error bug is fixed; all failed jobs now carry a real exception trace.

SECONDARY (NOT a hotfix regression — pre-existing slowness): Sonnet 4.5 + Haiku 4.5 both exceeded their per-model budgets for every test country (NZ/CA/SG). The hotfix did its job (surface the failure) but successful AI draft completion was never observed in this run. Investigate: (a) Is the Emergent LLM proxy adding latency? (b) Is the prompt too large for Haiku 45s? Consider shrinking prompt or raising Haiku budget to 60–75s. (c) Concurrent_request_limit on the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (test ran 3 AI jobs back-to-back).
Optional UX polish: when both models time out, the error message could include 'try again later' hint for admin UI consumers.
All NZ/CA/SG test workflows were terminal=failed so no DB pollution. AU TEST-189-hotfix workflow was created+verified+archived in test cleanup. Sir's PA-20260626-5F3E31 was NOT touched.
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: ai_workflow_service.call_ai_with_fallback correctly preserves last_err across both attempts, formats as 'type(last_err).__name__: last_err' which is exactly what surfaces in the job error column — verified live.

GOOD: country_workflows._execute_ai_draft outer wait_for=180s gives correct headroom over 135s worst case + 45s margin. Partial JSON fallback path (_partial_raw=True) is implemented but NOT exercised in this run since AI calls timed out before producing any text.

MINOR: In country_workflows.py line 513, the OUTER catch-all clause still uses str(e)[:300] without type prefix — inconsistent with the inner branch (line 435) which uses type(exc).__name__ + str(exc)[:400]. Make this consistent for forensic clarity. Low priority since the inner branch handles 99% of failures.

MINOR: AI_DRAFT_TIMEOUT=240s outer asyncio.wait_for at line 549–552 in ai_draft() is now redundant given the inner per-model + 180s wrap already bounds it at ~180s + bookkeeping. Could be lowered to 200s. Cosmetic.

OBSERVATION: Both Sonnet AND Haiku timed out at 45s for Haiku — but the log message shows 'exceeded 45s budget' for Haiku only, meaning Sonnet finished (within its 90s) then was overridden by an exception not visible in the trace, OR Sonnet itself timed out but the last_err got overwritten. Suggest enhancing the RuntimeError to include ALL attempts not just last: e.g. 'attempt_errors=[Sonnet: TimeoutError(90s), Haiku: TimeoutError(45s)]'. Better debuggability.
seed_data_creation: TEST-189-hotfix AU workflow created+verified, archived in cleanup. TEST-SMC-Resident-hotfix / TEST-EE-FSW-hotfix / TEST-EP-hotfix subclass_ids were submitted as AI draft jobs but jobs failed so no workflow rows created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: TC4 hotfix (empty error bug) is PROVEN FIXED via /app/backend/tests/test_sweep_b1_tc4_hotfix.py — re-run anytime with `pytest backend/tests/test_sweep_b1_tc4_hotfix.py -v -s`. Takes ~7 minutes (each AI draft test waits up to 4 min for terminal state). Live AI generation never succeeded in this env — but the hotfix's job is to surface that failure cleanly, which it does. If the next iteration sees AI completion succeed (faster Anthropic responses), the 'failed-path' branch of _validate_terminal_state will be skipped automatically — same test will still pass via the 'complete' branch.
rca of the issue: RCA validated end-to-end: ORIGINAL BUG = country_workflows.py wrapped call_ai_with_fallback in an INNER 90s wait_for then ran a retry loop, and when the outer asyncio.wait_for(90s) tripped, asyncio.TimeoutError() carries no message → str(exc) was '' → admin saw 'error=' empty in the failed job. HOTFIX = (a) push per-model timeouts INSIDE call_ai_with_fallback so Sonnet caps at 90s and Haiku at 45s and TimeoutError is replaced with TimeoutError('anthropic/<model> exceeded Xs budget') carrying a real message; (b) outer wrap is now 180s — well above 135s combined worst case; (c) error string composed as 'type(exc).__name__: str(exc)[:400]' — always non-empty. VERIFIED LIVE: 3 separate AI-draft jobs (NZ, CA, SG) all terminated as 'failed' with the EXACT meaningful string the hotfix promised, in ~138s each (well within 180s budget). No 5xx errors. No mystery hangs. Bug is dead.
